sourceMovie.title: Willow
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Willow works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Willow clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Maleficent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Willow clicked for you, Maleficent (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Willow?
faq.answer: Try The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring, Maleficent, The Beastmaster—each shares DNA with Willow while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Willow?
faq.answer: Willow (1988) is best described as Fantasy, Adventure, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Willow worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
